CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2012 Set For Trial On Oct-09-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
STEVEN BOOSKA, ESQ. OF THE LAW OFFICES OF STEVEN BOOSKA, TEL. 415.397.4345, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTY.
ARLO SMITH, ESQ. OF THE LAW OFFICES OF ARLO SMITH, TEL. 415.685.9331, APPEARING FOR PLANTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS CARLOS YANES and JUAN ESCOBAR's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT and DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS Granted. The defaults and default judgments as to both defendants are set aside and defendants are given ten days to file their answer. As to Mr. Yanes, while more than six months elapsed from the entry of default to the filing of the motion, the court has reviewed the papers filed in support of and in opposition to the motion and finds that the equitable doctrines of extrinsic fraud and/or extrinsic mistake are sufficient grounds to warrant the setting aside of both the default and default judgment. For purposes of this motion only, the court finds that Mr. Yanes reasonably relied on Mr. Garza's knowledge of the transfer of the debt to Mr. Escobar and Mr. Garza's acceptance of a large number of payments from Mr. Escobar and thus Mr. Yanes reasonably believed that he was no longer obligated on the debt, all of which prevented his responding to the complaint in a timely manner. As to Mr. Escobar, the court finds that he was not served with the summons and complaint and thus the time limits of Code of Civil Procedure 473 do not apply to him. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2011 Off Calendar

Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-28-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin; Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha; CSR: M. Baldocchi #5207.

Arlo Hale Smith, Esq. for and with Plaintiff: Mike Garza, was sworn and direct examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff: Mike Garza, and against the Defaulted Defendant(s): Carlos Yanes and Juan Escobar for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $288,000.00
Prejudgment Interest - $72,960.00
Attorney's Fees - per motion.
Property Taxes - $8,481.08

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
